title: New Zealand P-8A Poseidon Observes Possible DPRK Ship-to-Ship Sanctions Transfer
detail: New Zealand's Defence Force announced its P-8A Poseidon maritime surveillance aircraft observed a 'possible ship-to-ship transfer of illicit goods' in international waters near North Korea, among 35 'vessels of interest' identified in the Yellow Sea and East China Sea. The mission was part of the US-led Pacific Security Maritime Exchange monitoring operation, which coordinates multinational patrols to enforce UN sanctions on North Korea. China objected to the patrols, accusing New Zealand of 'close-in harassment' in China's exclusive economic zone, but Wellington dismissed the objections. The observation represents continued international enforcement of sanctions despite China's 2024 veto of the UN Panel of Experts mandate renewal, which eliminated the primary multilateral monitoring mechanism for DPRK sanctions.

media.caption: New Zealand P-8A Poseidon surveillance aircraft observed suspected DPRK sanctions violations at sea
